title: File.readChannel closes an unopened file on an early path
category: resource lifecycle (closing a file/channel that was never successfully opened)
crash_site: exception / spurious failure (e.g. on Linux, curl WebSocket timeouts) traced to File.readChannel closing a file handle that was not actually opened, in ktor-utils FileChannels.kt.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the close()/use{} call where the failure surfaces, and the RandomAccessFile/FileChannel open. The defect is readChannel's cleanup path closing a handle on an early return / before it was opened.
ground_truth.fix_summary: File.readChannel's failure/cleanup path could close a file that had not been opened (or close it twice) when an early return / exception occurred during setup, causing spurious failures. Fix: only close the file if it was actually opened (don't close an unopened file in readChannel).

admission.note: Authored from PR #5626 diff. crash != cause: the failure (e.g. curl WebSocket timeout on Linux) surfaces at the close/use site; the cause is readChannel's cleanup closing a handle it never opened on an early path. Decoy = the close call. Pure Kotlin-LANGUAGE instance (coroutine/JVM resource handling). Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/ktorio/ktor/pull/5626 — Don't close unopened file in readChannel; fix 94c346349a7c, base 3ccad96fbb89.
